titleStmt.title: Letter from William M. Nolan to Matthew Nathan, 7 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William M. Nolan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from the Town Clerk of Limerick William M. Nolan (1855-1941) to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) the under-secretary for Ireland. Nathan was under-secretary until the 1916 Rising after which he offered his resignation. The letter deals with the resignation of a Mr Patrick Reidy and particularly the amount of an allowance he will receive. The letter states that the council are considering a sum of £67:3:4 per year, and this is to be confirmed at the next Council meeting.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/6301

correspAction.placeName: Town Clerk's Office, Town Hall, Limerick, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Town Clerk's Office,
Town Hall,
Limerick. 7th April 1916.
5711.
"Local Officers Superannuation Act, 1869." Sir, I beg to inform you that your letter of the 3rd instant was
duly considered by the Gas Committee at a meeting held on the
6th instant, and the following resolution adopted unanimously :- "Resolved --- That having read the letter from the Under
Secretary of the 3rd instant intimating that the retiring
allowance which the Lord Lieutenant would be prepared to
sanction in the case of Mr. P. Reidy should be calculated on
the 31/60ths of his salary and emoluments at the date of his
retirement : that the resolution of the 29th ult be and is
hereby rescinded and that we recommend the County Borough
Council to grant the said Mr Patrick Reidy a Superranuation
Allowance of £67:5:4 a year (being 31/60ths of £2:10:0
a week, Ihis salary and emoluments at the date of his
retirement)." Please see paragraph II on enclosed copy Agenda. This resolution was unanimously adopted at the Quarterly
meeting of the Council held last evening, and I was directed to
ask His Excellency's approval thereof. It is proposed to confirm the proposal at the monthly meeting
of the Council on the 4th May next. As requested I enclose the Medical Certificate required to be
furnished by the Act. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant W. M. Nolan
Town Clerk. The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle. Await receipt of
confirming resolution
M.N.
10.4.16
